sutta: the buddha said monks i will teach you dhamma teaching dhamma teaching on crookedness or sneakiness do you listen to it pay attention carefully and i will speak we will sir reply those monks to the exalted 1 who said and what monks is dharma teaching which teaches about crookedness monks beings are the owners of the kama as to their karma born of their kama related to their kama abide supported by their kama whatever karma they do be it good or evil of that they will be the heirs in this connection monks a certain 1 takes life he is a hunter bloody handed given over to killing and slaying void of compassion to all living creatures he goes crookedly in body crookedly in speech crookedly in mind his action with body is crooked so is that with speech and mind crooked is his born and crooked he's rising up again in birth moreover monks for 1 who's born is crooked whose rebirth is crooked for him there is 1 of 2 bonds either downright war in hell or to be born in the womb of an animal 1 that creeps crookedly along and of what sword monks is that birth in the womb of an animal 1 that creeps crookedly along a snake a scorpion a centipede a mongoose a cat a mouse an owl or whatsoever other animal goes stealthily on seeing human beings here and again a certain 1 takes what is not given is a wrong doer in sensual desires is a liar a slanderer a bitter speech of idle babble coverages of harmful thoughts of wrong view of perverse view holding that there is no gift there is no gift no offering no sacrifice there is no fruit or ripening of deeds well done or ill done this world is not the world beyond is not there is no father no mother no being supernaturally born in other worlds there are no recluses and brahmanas or holy men in in the world who have gone the right way who fare rightly men who by their own comprehension have realized this world and the world beyond and declared us he goes crookedly in body speech and mind his actions with body speech and mind is crooked he's born is crooked for 1 who's born is crooked for him i declare that is 1 of 2 bonds either downright war in hell or to be born in the womb of an animal a snake a scorpion a centipede a mongoose a mouse a cat an owl or whatsoever other animal goes stealthily on seeing human beings monks beings are the owners of their kama as to their kama born of the kama related to their kama abide supported by their kama whatever kama they do be it good or evil of that it will be the heirs herein again monks a certain 1 abandons taking life abstains there from he lays aside the rod he lays aside the night he dwells modest charitable feeling compassion for all living creatures he goes not crookedly in body speech and mind his action with body speech and mind is straight forward is his born and straight his rebirth now monks for 1 who's born and rebirth are straight i declare 1 of 2 bonds either those heaven worlds that are utter bliss or rebirth in whatsoever families are exalted such as the families of nobles or brahmins or house fathers of great household wealthy of great resources of great property with great store of gold and silver with great straw possessions with great straw wealth and grain thus become monks is the rebirth of 1 who has come to be what he does by then he is reborn when reborn appropriate context contact such a 1 thus i declare that beings are the heirs of their deeds herein again monks a certain 1 abandons taking what is not given he abstains there from he abandons wrong action in sensual desires he abandons falsehood slander call speech or bitter speech cover thing he is of harmless thoughts he has right view he holds the reasonable view that there is gift that there is offering sacrifice that there is fruit and ripening of deeds done well or ill that this world is that the world beyond is that there are mother and father and being supernaturally born in other worlds that in this world there are recluses and brahmins who go the right way who fare rightly who by their own comprehension have realized both this world and the world beyond and so declared such a 1 goes not crookedly in body speech and mind his action of body speech and mind is straight outright straightforward is his born and straight his rebirth now monks for 1 who's born is straight i declare 1 of 2 bonds either those heavens that are utter bliss or rebirth in what so families are exalted with great store of wealth and grain etc indeed monks beings are the owners of the karma heirs of the kama born of the kama related to their kama abide supported by their kama whatever karma they do bit good or evil of that they will be the heirs such monks is the dhamma teaching the teaching about crookedness and that's the whole suta
commentary: so here the buddha is saying and whatever nature we have we will be reborn in the same way so if a person is a crooked is [Music] 1 who is very secretive he grows stealthily longer he might be born as an animal that is of such a nature a snake scorpion centipede mongoose cat mouse owl such type of creature and if he and there's a lot of evil deeds he might even be reborn in hell here is mentioned the 10 deeds which are evil kama 3 of the body 3 body evil karma is killing stealing and committing adultery these are the 3 evil body commas and then the verb evil verbal karmas are 4 types the first 1 is lying the second 1 is carrying tails from a to b and making uh a and b quarrel a person hears a talking bad about b and then he goes to tell b yeah a a says i said such a thing about you so b gets very angry and quarrels with a or fights with air so he causes this harmony by carrying tales that's the second type of evil verbal karma the third type of evil verbal karma is called speech or harsh speech using foul words swearing and all that always talking very heatedly very angrily like that the fourth time of evil a verbal karma is idle gossip idol gossip talking a lot of nonsense frivolous words so these are the 4 uh evil karmas 4 verbal evil kamas then there are 3 types of evil mental karmas the first 1 is being over greedy covetousness that means greedy for other people's property the second 1 is ill will having a lot of anger and hatred always having a lot of ill will third 1 is having wrong view having wrong view means not believing in karma believing that if you do good you will get happiness that you do evil you will suffer for it and also not believing that there are holy men who have become enlightened and realize the truth about the nature of the world and not believing that there is another world after you pass away there are heavenly beings and all other types of beings in the other world so the 10 good karmas are the opposite of the 10 evil karmas and refraining from killing refraining from stealing refraining from sexual misconduct this adultery refraining from lying refraining from caring tales and causing disharmony refraining from core speech refraining from idol gossip and then the opposite of covetousness which is renunciation not being greedy enunciation then another 1 is not having ill will having a lot of good will having a lot of loving kindness as a as the ninth good karma and the tenth 1 is having right view believing in karma believing that there is another world after you pass away believing that there are holy men who have realized the truth so the buddha says if we perform the 10 evil karmas we will be reborn the chances are that we will be reborn either in hell or as an animal and if you do the 10 good karmas perform the 10 good karmas then the chances are that you may be reborn in heaven or at least as a human being let me go to the next suta
